form.label: Admin: People & Defaults
form.description: Add or edit a person's registry row — display name, per-person default units + goals, height — and map a Splunk login to a person_id (the identity map). Admin-only: writes go to the wearable_person_profile KV Store (locked to admin/sc_admin) via the risky-command (outputlookup) approve dialog.

input.label: Confirm write
input.choice: Yes — write this person to the registry

search.query: | inputlookup wearable_person_profile | eval label=person_id." — ".coalesce(person_name, person_id) | sort person_id | table person_id label

input.label: Confirm remove
input.choice: Yes — permanently remove this person's registry row

div.style: border:2px solid #F4A422; background:rgba(244,164,34,0.12); border-radius:6px; padding:12px 14px; margin-bottom:14px; color:#F4A422; font-weight:600

div.div: ⚠ After you click Submit, Splunk shows a small "potential security risk" dialog (it approves the outputlookup command). On the dark theme it's easy to miss — look near the center of the screen and click "Run Query Anyway" to proceed. Nothing is written until you finish the fields (including Confirm), click Submit, and approve.
div.p: This page maintains the wearable_person_profile registry (KV Store) — the human layer over the opaque, RBAC-gated person_id. Writes are locked to admin/sc_admin; a non-admin who reaches this page can view the roster but any write returns a permission error.

ul.li: Add / Edit — type the person_id. A new id creates the row; an existing id edits it. Every field left blank keeps its current value (read-modify-write), so you only fill what you want to change.

Splunk username is the identity map ($env:user$ → person_id) — this person's own Splunk login, used to auto-select the logged-in user + their default units. It must be unique (one login = one person); a write reusing a username already mapped elsewhere is blocked and the registry is left unchanged. Leave it blank for people who don't log in themselves — children, elders, or a coworker whose device you ingest. A caregiver is granted access to those dependents via their role's srchFilter (RBAC: person_id IN (…)), not this field.
Default units / goals / height are the per-person display defaults the dashboards read.
Remove deletes a person's registry row (does not delete their indexed data — use Admin: Sample Data → Clean up for synthetic events).
To seed a brand-new person's demo data after adding them here, use Admin: Sample Data → Create.

search.query: | inputlookup wearable_person_profile | eval person_name=coalesce(person_name, person_id) | sort person_id | table person_id splunk_user person_name units step_goal calorie_goal distance_goal_m height_cm

| inputlookup wearable_person_profile where person_id="$p_id$" | appendpipe [| stats count | where count==0 | eval person_id="$p_id$"] | eval splunk_user = if("$p_user$"!="", "$p_user$", splunk_user) | eval person_name = if("$p_name$"!="", "$p_name$", person_name) | eval units = if("$p_units$"!="", "$p_units$", units) | eval _step = if("$p_step$"=="", "0", "$p_step$") | eval _cal = if("$p_cal$"=="", "0", "$p_cal$") | eval _dist = if("$p_dist$"=="", "0", "$p_dist$") | eval _height = if("$p_height$"=="", "0", "$p_height$") | eval step_goal = if("$p_step$"=="", step_goal, tonumber(_step)) | eval calorie_goal = if("$p_cal$"=="", calorie_goal, tonumber(_cal)) | eval distance_goal_m = if("$p_dist$"=="", distance_goal_m, tonumber(_dist)) | eval height_cm = if("$p_height$"=="", height_cm, tonumber(_height)) | eval person_id=coalesce(person_id,"$p_id$") | where person_id!="" | join type=left splunk_user [| inputlookup wearable_person_profile | where isnotnull(splunk_user) AND splunk_user!="" | stats values(person_id) as holders by splunk_user] | eval others=mvfilter(holders!=person_id) | eval blocked=if(splunk_user!="" AND mvcount(others)>0, 1, 0) | appendpipe [| where blocked=0 | table _key person_id splunk_user person_name units step_goal calorie_goal distance_goal_m height_cm | outputlookup append=t wearable_person_profile | where 1=2] | eval outcome=if(blocked=1, "BLOCKED: Splunk username already maps to ".mvjoin(others,", ")." (registry left unchanged)", "written") | table outcome person_id splunk_user person_name units step_goal calorie_goal distance_goal_m height_cm
| inputlookup wearable_person_profile | where person_id!="$r_id$" | outputlookup wearable_person_profile | stats count as rows_remaining
